Gareth Murphy
AboutGalleryProjects LinkedIn GitHub

About

I am a Principal Data Steward at Novo Nordisk, Denmark, where I lead teams focused on making life science data Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable (FAIR).

What I Do

I work at the intersection of scientific computing, data management, and engineering — building the infrastructure that enables researchers to find, access, and reuse experimental data at scale.

  • FAIR Data & Open Science — designing metadata standards, data catalogs, and automated ingestion pipelines for experimental data
  • Research Data Management — leading development of data management platforms including SciCat, an open-source scientific data catalog
  • High-Performance Computing — years of experience with MPI-parallel simulation codes for astrophysical phenomena
  • Team Leadership — building and mentoring cross-functional engineering teams across research and IT

Background

My career spans scientific research and software engineering. I hold a PhD in astrophysics and have worked at institutions including the European Spallation Source (ESS), the Technical University of Denmark (DTU), and Novo Nordisk.

Earlier in my career I developed MHD simulation codes for modelling jets and supernovae, and FPGA-based digital signal processing filters. I now apply that same rigour to data engineering challenges in the life sciences.

Skills

  • Languages: Python, C++, Bash, JavaScript/TypeScript
  • Data & Metadata: HDF5, NeXus, JSON-LD, DCAT, schema.org
  • Platforms: Kubernetes, Docker, GitHub Actions, AWS
  • Tools: Jupyter, Elasticsearch, MongoDB, RabbitMQ
